---
title: "ai-minerals"
subtitle: "Mineral prospectivity mapping on public open data"
---

This site is a portfolio. Each chapter asks, on a different geological
problem or methodological angle, the same question: how far can a
careful machine-learning pipeline trained on public data go toward
narrowing where to drill, and where does the methodology break?

The work covers four regional pipelines (Eastern Alaska porphyry-Cu,
BC Golden Triangle multi-deposit, California Mother Lode orogenic-Au,
Arizona porphyry-Cu), independent reproductions of two canonical
published methods (Lawley 2022 continental Zn-Pb; DEEP-SEAM Curnamona
REE), a five-region cross-region experiment testing whether published
methods transfer across deposit types and provinces, and
decision-theoretic drill-planning work at both regional and property
scale. Underneath each chapter sits a deeper notebook trail covering
data, methodology, code, and limitations
([review build](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/)).

## Chapters

### [1. Bear Cub (Cape Nome placer Au)](bear_cub.qmd)

Family-heritage mineral patent on the Nome paleo-beach gold deposits.
Twenty-four 1919-1955 paper drill logs ingested with a multi-engine
OCR pipeline (Tesseract, TrOCR, Cloud Vision, Claude Opus with
structured-output JSON arbitration), reconstructed as a
three-dimensional model of bedrock surface + pay-zone horizon, and
resourced with Voronoi-cell volumetrics + Monte Carlo uncertainty.

### [2. Regional MPM pipelines](regional.qmd)

Four within-region prospectivity pipelines built on the same
methodology: USGS / NRCan / state-survey public data → Random Forest
with 20 km spatial-block CV → leak-free out-of-fold scoring with
bootstrap CIs. Top-1% capture rates of 22-23% across the four
regions, 22-23× enrichment over random.

### [3. Reproducing + auditing two published methods](reproductions.qmd)

Cloned and re-ran the official code for two canonical MPM
publications. **Lawley 2022** (continental Zn-Pb, AUC 0.983
published) reproduces cleanly but the published number includes a
label leak (2.4 pp), a 1-D vs 2-D spatial-blocking gap (9.0 pp), and
a cross-continent transfer collapse (~16 pp). Honest AUC: 0.71-0.87.
**DEEP-SEAM** (Curnamona REE, 86% top-2% capture published) is
measured under a 0-D random split with no spatial blocking; the
implementation also diverges from the paper text in two places.

### [4. Cross-region experiments](cross_region.qmd)

Five-region transferability test for the DEEP-SEAM deviation-network
architecture. The published 2-3× advantage over Random Forest holds
on its tuning dataset (Curnamona REE, 7 positives) and on nothing
else: Tanacross porphyry-Cu (45 positives), Arizona porphyry-Cu (191
positives), Lawley continental MVT (2,027 positives, 2-D blocked CV),
and US Western Carbonatite REE Belt (14 positives, within-niche test)
all favor classical tree-based methods by 7 to 37 percentage points.

### [5. Drill planning under uncertainty](drill_planning.qmd)

The downstream question — given a prospectivity map, where should
the next hole go? — at two scales. A POMDP framing with Monte Carlo
Tree Search on the BCGT regional prior, plus a Gaussian-process
drill-site recommender on the Bear Cub placer property. Two
different mathematical machineries, same intellectual move from
probability surface to hole-location decision.

## Methodology in one paragraph

The methodology is standard: federal and state agencies publish
mineral occurrence records, geologic maps, geophysical surveys,
stream-sediment geochemistry, and remote-sensing imagery. Resampled
onto a common grid, each cell of land becomes a feature vector. A
Random Forest (or gradient-boosted trees, or a deep deviation
network) is trained to recognize cells that contain a known deposit,
holding out spatial blocks so it can't memorize neighbors. The
output is a per-cell score, mapped back across the region: where the
model thinks the unknown deposits are.

What separates a portfolio model from an academic exercise is what
happens after the score map is drawn. The methodology has four
well-known traps — training-positive inflation, spatial leakage,
exploration bias in the labels, cross-region transfer failure — and
all four show up in the work below. The audit work in Chapter 3
quantifies how much of two canonical published AUC numbers comes
from each trap.

## Stack

USGS, NRCan, state-survey, and Sentinel-2 public data feeds. Python
plus scikit-learn, pandas + pyarrow, geopandas + rasterio, PyTorch
(for the DEEP-SEAM PyTorch port), PyVista (for Bear Cub 3-D
visualization), Quarto (for this site).

## Site map

| Chapter | Detail |
|---|---|
| [1. Bear Cub](bear_cub.qmd) | [internal/bear_cub/main](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/bear_cub/main.html), [mining_review](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/bear_cub/mining_review.html) |
| [2. Regional MPM](regional.qmd) | [Eastern Alaska](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/eastak/eastak_porphyry_prospectivity.html), [BCGT](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/bcgt/bcgt_porphyry_prospectivity.html), [Mother Lode](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/motherlode.html), [Arizona](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/arizona/main.html) |
| [3. Reproductions + audits](reproductions.qmd) | [Lawley](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/lawley.html), [Lawley audit](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/lawley_audit.html), [DEEP-SEAM](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/deep_seam_cross_region.html), [DEEP-SEAM audit](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/deep_seam_audit.html) |
| [4. Cross-region](cross_region.qmd) | [four-cell deep dive](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/cross_region/four_cell.html), [US Carbonatite REE](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/prospectus/us_carbonatite_ree.html) |
| [5. Drill planning](drill_planning.qmd) | [overview](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/drill_planning/index.html), [BCGT POMDP](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/bcgt/decision_planning.html), [Bear Cub GP](https://johnsondevco.com/plans/ai-minerals-internal/notebooks/drill_planning/bear_cub_gp_recommender.html) |

## Reproduce

```bash
git clone <this-repo>
cd ai-minerals
uv sync
quarto render
```

License: MIT.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from datetime import date
print(f"\n---\n\n*Site rendered {date.today().isoformat()}.*")
```
